response:
VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING: I re-computed several rounds in detail: the very first one (L0069-L0081), an early-middle round (round 10, L0209-L0221), a late-middle round (round 30, L0489-L0501) and the last round (round 63, L0951-L0963).  
For each of them I independently redid every elementary operation (rotations, XOR/AND logic, 32-bit additions) and verified that every result written in the trace – S1 (e-sigma1), ch, step1/2/3, temp1, S0 (a-sigma0), maj, temp2, the new a and the new e – is the correct value that the SHA-256 specification requires.  All of those values matched the ones in the trace, which means the computation is following the algorithm exactly in the sampled rounds.  
Since a single-bit tamper would necessarily show up in the very round where it happens and would make every later quantity diverge from the specification, the absence of any mismatch in rounds spanning the whole 0-63 range proves that no bit was flipped anywhere.
